titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to Michael Collins, 29 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Carey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Patrick Carey (1853-1934) was the Union Clerk for the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians. Poor law unions and their workhouses were established by the Poor Law (Ireland) Act, 1838, as a response to widespread poverty, and were each governed by a Board of Guardians. This letter comes from the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians Minute Book.

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
29th Nov. 1915 Dear Sir, I am directed by the Board of Guardians to
inform you that they have accepted your tender for the
supply of 4tons of hay at Four Pounds Five Shillings
(£4.5.0) per ton. Please have four tons supplied with as little
delay as possible. Yours faithfully, Clerk of Union. Mr. Michael Collins,
Urrohogal,
Farramfore. PS Kindly attend here with your
supplies as soon as possible to
perfect bond for supplying hay
PC.
